titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas and Ellen Leonard to Cornelius Daly, 15 March 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Leonard

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: No 4 Main Street, Athlone, Westmeath, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
No 4 Main St
Athlone
Co Westmeath 15th March 1923 Dear Mr & Mrs Daly It was with the deepest regret and
amazement that I read to-days paper
and saw the official report of your noble
son's execution - murder is the proper name
for it - and I, with my wife, hasten
to convey to you, our most heartfelt
& profound sympathy in your terrible
loss. May God have Mercy on his soul. Charlie was my senior officer whilst
opperating in the North & many a time
he & I were bedfellows having stayed with
me at Dungannon when first he went there. After escaping from Kilkenny jail I
- owing to certain difficulties - came to Athlone
my native town, & opened business. Again
tendering our heartfelt sympathy I remain yours sincerely
Thomas & Ellen Leonard
